topic: MemPalace: The Highest-Scoring Free AI Memory System for Agents

key_points: Mem0 uses LLM-based extraction — reads conversations and extracts 'key facts', discarding the rest
Costs $19-249/mo for cloud version, open-source self-hosted option available
~2,000 tokens required at startup vs MemPalace's 170 tokens
Scores ~85% on LongMemEval vs MemPalace's 96.6%
Fully managed cloud infrastructure with enterprise SLAs
Vectorize provides critical independent review of MemPalace's benchmark claims
Notes that 96.6% score measures ChromaDB embedding performance, not Palace architecture
Highlights that 100% hybrid score was achieved via hand-coded patches on failing test cases
Recommends MemPalace only for local-only zero-cost experiments with maturity caveats
Zep uses Graphiti engine to build a temporal knowledge graph tracking when facts were true
Zep costs $25/mo+ and is SaaS-only
Offers SOC2/HIPAA compliance for enterprise use cases
Strong for complex Neo4j-based knowledge graphs
LangMem integrates natively with LangChain/LangGraph ecosystem
MemoClaw targets multi-agent orchestration scenarios
Most alternatives require cloud infrastructure or significant setup
MemPalace stands out for pure local deployment with zero API costs
Independent reviewer: real innovation in verbatim storage philosophy, questionable benchmark claims
Pre-tuning 96.6% is the honest number; 100% hybrid score violates benchmark integrity
Architecture inspired by ancient memory-palace mnemonic technique
Core insight: keep everything verbatim, let semantic search surface it

stats.fact: MemPalace achieves 96.6% Recall@5 on LongMemEval benchmark — highest score among free AI memory tools
MemPalace launched April 5, 2026 and gained 7,000+ GitHub stars within 48 hours
By April 8, 2026, MemPalace crossed 23,000 GitHub stars and ~3,000 forks, becoming #1 trending repo on GitHub
MemPalace requires only 170 tokens at startup vs Mem0's ~2,000 — 10x reduction in context window usage
AAAK compression achieves 30x compression ratio — 6-month history (~19.5M tokens) compresses to ~650K tokens
AAAK mode scores 84.2% vs raw verbatim mode's 96.6% on LongMemEval
MemPalace exposes 19-29 MCP tools for AI clients including Claude Code: search, storage, knowledge graph, and agent diaries
Mem0 cloud costs $19-249/mo; Zep costs $25/mo+; MemPalace is completely free and MIT licensed
MemPalace supports 50+ simultaneous agents with zero performance degradation

angles: Celebrity-backed open source: Milla Jovovich & Ben Sigman's unexpected entry into AI tooling
Philosophy of 'store everything verbatim' vs AI extraction — why discarding context harms recall accuracy
Benchmark controversy deep dive: why 96.6% is real and 100% is not — what this reveals about AI memory benchmarking
Privacy-first AI memory: zero API calls, fully local ChromaDB + SQLite, MIT licensed
Token efficiency: 170 tokens vs 2,000 tokens at startup — massive savings for production agents
MCP ecosystem angle: MemPalace as a drop-in memory layer for Claude Code, Cursor, ChatGPT
The ancient method of loci meets modern LLMs: how spatial metaphors structure AI memory
Developer adoption vs skepticism: 47,000 GitHub stars vs accusations of purchased stars and 'ChromaDB with a celebrity name'
